Let me check if there's a way to reach the target from the white corridors. The white corridor at cols 32-38 goes from rows 14-22 (approximately). If the player can reach this corridor, maybe there's a path to the target.
But the player is at cols 14-18, and the white corridor is at cols 32-38. To reach the white corridor, the player needs to move RIGHT through the black wall at cols 19-31.
Actually, looking more carefully at the maze structure, the black areas (B) are the maze walls, and the white areas (W) are the corridors. The player is in a black area, which suggests the player is in a wall, not a corridor. This is confusing.
Let me re-examine the maze structure. The player moves in a black corridor at cols 14-18. The white areas at cols 20-24 and cols 32-38 are also corridors, but they're white.
I think the key insight is that the player can only move in black corridors, not white corridors. The white corridors are "open" areas that the player can't enter.
But wait, the player just moved DOWN from rows 8-12 to rows 14-18, and the player is now at rows 14-18, cols 14-18, which is still in the black corridor. So the player can move in black corridors.
Let me check if the player can move RIGHT from the current position. The player is at cols 14-18, and col 19 is B (black). So the player might be able to move RIGHT into the black wall.
Actually, I think the black areas are the corridors, and the white areas are walls. The player moves in black corridors, and the white areas are obstacles.
